description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight, tapered blade made of metallic material, likely steel, giving it a silvery-gray appearance. The blade is symmetrical and gradually narrows to a sharp point, suitable for thrusting and slashing. The hilt features a simple, straight crossguard in a dark color, providing hand protection and balance. The handle is cylindrical and metallic, ending in a rounded pommel with a gold or brass finish, which adds a touch of contrast and may serve as a counterweight. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation, suggesting functionality over decoration. The sword's proportions and construction indicate it is designed for one-handed or possibly hand-and-a-half use, typical of medieval European swords.
description: A long, straight, double-edged sword with a metallic blade, dark crossguard, and a gold-tipped cylindrical handle. The design is simple and functional, with minimal decoration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight, tapered blade and the cylindrical handle with a gold pommel.
description_view_1: Blade-on view highlighting the flat, double-edged geometry and the width of the crossguard.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the full length of the blade and handle, with the gold pommel visible.
description_view_3: Handle-on view showing the circular cross-section of the handle and the symmetrical crossguard.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical metallic handle with a rounded gold pommel
